assertion.title: sin2/mulsin/mulcos/mulnegexp absorption operators reduce total nodes 283→275
assertion.statement: Adding compound operators that absorb mul(X, f(Y)) patterns — mulsin(a,b)=a*sin(b), mulcos(a,b)=a*cos(b), mulnegexp(a,b)=a*exp(-b) — plus unary sin2(x)=sin(2x) saves 8 nodes across 7 equations while maintaining 50/50 exact matches.

details.hypothesis: Binary compound operators absorbing mul(X, unary_op(Y)) patterns and the sin(2x) pattern will reduce total node count by compressing the common 'scaled function' pattern in physics equations.
changes: Added sin2 unary operator: sin2(x) = sin(2x)
Added mulsin binary operator: mulsin(a,b) = a*sin(b)
Added mulcos binary operator: mulcos(a,b) = a*cos(b)
Added mulnegexp binary operator: mulnegexp(a,b) = a*exp(-b)
Added simplification rules: sin(mul(2,X))→sin2(X), mul(X,sin(Y))→mulsin(X,Y), mul(X,cos(Y))→mulcos(X,Y), mul(X,negexp(Y))→mulnegexp(X,Y) with commuted variants
Updated permutation templates for eq33, eq38, eq39, eq42, eq43, eq49 to use new operators directly

per_equation_savings.eq36_snells_law: 7→6 (arcsin(n1*sin(θ1)/n2) → arcsin(n1 mulsin θ1 / n2), bonus from mulsin simplification)
